title: Understanding Acceleration
text: Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity over time. It can be calculated using the formula: acceleration $$=$$ (final velocity - initial velocity) / time.

title: Identify Known Values
text: From the problem, we know the initial velocity is $$0$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$ (since the cheetah starts from rest), the final velocity is $$30.0$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$, and the time taken is $$7.00$$ s.

title: Calculate the Acceleration
text: Use the formula: acceleration $$=$$ (final velocity - initial velocity) / time. Substitute the known values: acceleration $$=$$ $$\frac{30-0}{7}$$.
